title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Success in professional sports is (0) UNDENIABLY (DENY) linked to mental strength. Athletes often face (17) REPEATED (REPEAT) pressure to perform at their best under extreme conditions. Experts argue that (18) CONFIDENCE (CONFIDE) is just as important as physical training. Many stars use (19) VISUALISATION (VISUAL) techniques to prepare for major competitions. This mental rehearsal (20) STRENGTHENS (STRONG) their focus and reduces anxiety before a race. However, the (21) UNCERTAINTY (CERTAIN) of competition means that even the most prepared can fail. Developing (22) RESILIENCE (RESILIENT) is therefore crucial for bouncing back after a loss. Coaches emphasize the (23) NECESSITY (NECESSARY) of maintaining a positive mindset regardless of the score. The (24) GROWTH (GROW) of sports psychology has transformed how modern teams approach their daily routines. It provides them with the tools needed to overcome internal obstacles and achieve peak performance on the field, ensuring that the mind is as ready as the body for the challenges ahead.

example.text: Success in professional sports is (0) UNDENIABLY (DENY) linked...
example.explanation: Adverb with a negative prefix required; DENY → UNDENIABLY.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'pressure'; REPEAT → REPEATED.
Noun required as the subject; CONFIDE → CONFIDENCE.
Noun required for the technique; VISUAL → VISUALISATION.
Verb in present simple third person required; STRONG → STRENGTHENS.
Noun with a negative prefix required; CERTAIN → UNCERTAINTY.
Noun required after the verb 'developing'; RESILIENT → RESILIENCE.
Noun required after the definite article; NECESSARY → NECESSITY.
